course_description.overview: A three-day alpine cycling event combining road cycling and mountain biking in the Mont Blanc massif. The Gran Fondo component features a 1 or 2-day format on Tour de France legendary mountain passes with multiple distance options for all levels.

key_segments.name: Col de Joux Plane
key_segments.description: Iconic Tour de France climb featured in the Gran Fondo route

key_segments.name: Col de la Ramaz
key_segments.description: Iconic Tour de France climb featured in the Gran Fondo route

key_segments.name: Colombière
key_segments.description: Iconic Tour de France climb featured in the Gran Fondo route

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Described as a demanding but accessible alpine cyclosportive experience combining performance with shared pleasure. The event features multiple distance options for all levels, from beginners to experienced riders, with emphasis on total immersion on Tour de France roads.

logistics.getting_there: Located in Haute-Savoie in the French Alps near Mont Blanc massif. Specific transportation details not provided in available sources.
logistics.accommodation: Accommodation packages available through event organizers. Lodging options can be booked through the official Gran Fondo & Ultra Raid website.
logistics.registration_details: Event offers 1 or 2-day stage format options with several distances for all levels. Mass start format. Registration available through official event channels.
logistics.bike_logistics: Road cycling event requiring road or gravel-capable bikes suitable for alpine terrain and mountain passes.
logistics.local_considerations: Three-day festival atmosphere with concerts, entertainment, exhibitors' village, workshops, cycling initiation courses for children and beginners, and discovery activities showcasing valley attractions.

final_verdict.one_liner: A well-organized alpine Gran Fondo combining legendary Tour de France passes with festival atmosphere, ideal for riders seeking challenging mountain cycling in an accessible, community-focused format.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Cyclists of all levels seeking alpine road cycling experiences on iconic Tour de France terrain. Families and groups looking for a multi-day cycling festival with non-competitive activities. Riders wanting to experience demanding but accessible mountain passes in a supportive environment.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, particularly for those seeking authentic alpine cycling on legendary passes with strong community atmosphere and festival elements. The three-day format with multiple distance options and non-cycling activities makes it valuable for groups with mixed abilities.
pro_tips: Book accommodation early through official event channels as lodging fills quickly during the June event window
Choose the 1 or 2-day format based on fitness level and time availability; multiple distance options available for all abilities
Arrive early to experience the full festival atmosphere including concerts, workshops, and exhibitors' village
Prepare for alpine weather conditions and ensure bike is properly serviced for sustained climbing on technical mountain passes
